Title: inDrive introduces 1.0pct commission rate for KL drivers
Article:
inDrive has implemented a one per cent commission rate for e-hailing drivers in Kuala Lumpur, enabling them to keep 99 per cent of their fares throughout the campaign period ending May 31.
The mobility and delivery platform said the initiative is available to both new and existing drivers, providing a different earnings structure as operating costs rise.
The standard commission rate for e-hailing in the country is legally capped at 20 per cent of the driver's fare.
For taxis using e-hailing apps, the cap is set at 10 per cent.
While inDrive's temporary one per cent commission is a significant outlier designed for a limited campaign period, most major competitors operate near the legal maximum or offer tiered incentives
inDrive said increasing fuel prices, influenced by global market uncertainties, have added pressure to drivers' daily expenses and net income.
"For many e-hailing drivers, fuel, insurance, and vehicle maintenance are unavoidable expenses. These costs directly impact their take-home income.
"In this environment, platform commission structures play an increasingly critical role in determining whether gig work remains sustainable. The new initiative is designed to respond to these realities," the company said.
inDrive Malaysia driver operations manager Azlan Anwar said fair commission structures and transparent pricing are necessary for the long-term sustainability of gig work, especially in Malaysia where the sector remains a primary income source for many.
"With this initiative, we are putting more earnings back into the hands of drivers and reinforcing our commitment to a fairer, more transparent model.
"Drivers are the backbone of the e-hailing ecosystem, and we believe our driver partners deserve a fairer share of the value they create," he said.
The company noted that it has consistently maintained low commission rates in the Malaysian market.
It operates using a peer-to-peer pricing model, which allows drivers and passengers to negotiate fares directly rather than relying on automated algorithm-based pricing.
The platform said this approach is intended to provide increased flexibility, transparency, and control for both parties involved in the transaction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ipoh City Council clears vagrants from shoplot walkways
Article:
IPOH: The Ipoh City Council (MBI) conducted an operation on Tuesday to clear and sanitise shoplot walkways following
viral videos of vagrants sleeping
in the area.
The operation targeted walkways around Jalan Dato Onn Jaafar and Jalan Foo Yet Kai.
An MBI spokesman said the exercise involved 24 personnel, including officers from the Social Welfare Department and the National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada).
Eight people underwent urine tests, with one person handed over to Nada for further action. The remaining vagrants were placed under the custody of the Welfare Department.
The spokesman said the operation focused on enforcement and cleanliness while addressing vagrancy through comprehensive and humane social intervention.
"This proactive step aims to tackle the issue of vagrants in public spaces while ensuring the city remains clean, organised, and safe for the community," the spokesman said in a statement.
Cleaning teams from the MBI Health Department used an open-top lorry and a water tanker to sanitise the affected locations.
Vagrants in the city are currently referred to Anjung Singgah Perak in Jalan Medan Istana 1.
Established by the National Welfare Foundation, the centre provides free temporary accommodation, counselling, food, and welfare assistance to help people improve their lives and develop new skills.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heavy rain, thunderstorm warning in 11 states and KL
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for 11 states and Kuala Lumpur, with heavy rain and strong winds, until 7pm today.
Areas expected to be affected are Perlis, Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu); Penang (Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan) and Perak.
In Kelantan, the affected areas are Pasir Mas, Jeli, Tanah Merah, Machang, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang); Terengganu (Besut and Hulu Terengganu); Pahang (Tanah Tinggi Cameron, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Bera and Rompin)and Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat).
The warning also applies to Kuala Lumpur, Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin) and Johor (Segamat, Batu Pahat, Mersing, Kota Tinggi and Johor Baru).
In Sarawak, the areas are Kuching (Bau and Kuching), Serian, Samarahan (Simunjan), Sri Aman, Betong, Sarikei (Pakan, Julau and Meradong), Sibu, Miri (Telang Usan) and Limbang.
According to the department, the warning is issued when there are signs of thunderstorm activity with rainfall exceeding 20 millimetres per hour, either occurring or expected to persist for more than an hour.
MetMalaysia said the short-term warning is valid for a period not exceeding six hours from the time it is issued.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rohingya sea crossings hit record death toll in 2025, UNHCR says
Article:
GENEVA: Nearly 900 Rohingya refugees were reported missing or dead in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal in 2025, making it the deadliest year on record for the route, the United Nations refugee agency said on Friday.
More than one in seven of the estimated 6,500 Rohingya refugees who attempted the sea crossing last year were reported missing or dead, marking the highest mortality rate worldwide for refugee and migrant sea journeys, UNHCR spokesperson Babar Baloch told reporters in Geneva.
—Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Viral bus "lap" incident woman, 8As SPM achiever, pleads to continue studies
Article:
JASIN: The woman in a viral video showing her sitting on the lap of an express bus driver - Nur Adila Najwa Ilham Ameerrullah, 21 - told the magistrate's court here today that she hopes to continue her studies.
She made the statement after she and her boyfriend, Arif Fahmi Abd Salam, 36, pleaded guilty to charges arising from the recent viral express bus incident.
Magistrate Mazana Sinin sentenced Arif to one year's jail and a RM5,000 fine, while Nur Adila Najwa was sentenced to six months' jail and a RM5,000 fine after both pleaded guilty when the charges were read.
Nur Adila Najwa said in mitigation that she had been raised by her grandmother since infancy and later faced financial difficulties following her grandmother's death, which led her to seek employment in Kuala Lumpur.
She told the court she had previously received opportunities to further her studies but was unable to pursue them due to financial constraints, although she had achieved 8As and 2Bs in her Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examinations three years ago.
"I admit guilt. I am only 21 years old and have lived with my grandmother since I was a baby," she said.
"I scored 8As and 2Bs in my SPM, but had to decline offers to further my studies due to financial difficulties.
"After my grandmother passed away, I had no one to support me, so I moved to Kuala Lumpur to work and hope to continue my studies," she added.
Her co-accused, former express bus driver Arif Fahmi Abd Salam, 36, also appealed for leniency, saying he had lost his job following the circulation of the video and was left without income.
The court also ordered an additional two months' jail in default of payment of fines and disqualified both from holding or obtaining a driving licence for five years.
Both were jointly charged with driving a bus bearing registration number APE 9588 in a manner dangerous to the public.
The offence was alleged to have occurred at the exit of the Bemban Rest and Recreation (R&R) area along the North-South Expressway southbound in Jasin at 7.30pm on April 12.
The charge was framed under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Amendment 2020), read together with Section 34 of the Penal Code.
In separate proceedings, Nur Adila Najwa also faced a charge under Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act 1955 for allegedly using abusive language and insulting a 44-year-old female passenger aboard the same bus, causing distress at 6pm on the same day and location.
The offence carries a maximum fine of RM100 if convicted.
Prosecutors Mohd Asri Abd Rahman Sidik and Syazwani Mohamad Yusri urged the court to impose a deterrent sentence, stating that the offence reflected serious disregard for road safety and public safety concerns, and involved the potential risk to 27 passengers on board the bus.
They said such conduct on public transport was unacceptable and warranted a firm response from the court.
After hearing submissions from both sides, the court imposed the sentences, with all custodial sentences to run from the date of conviction.
Nur Adila Najwa was also fined RM100 or 14 days' jail in default under the Minor Offences Act charge.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 6 injured as motorcycles collide during street racing crackdown, one catches fire
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: Six motorcycles collided while fleeing an illegal street racing crackdown, leaving one bike burnt and six riders injured on Friday.
The incident happened at Jalan Tengku Mizan near the Kampung Tebauk traffic lights in Kuala Nerus, involving riders aged between 16 and 21.
Kuala Terengganu district police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the incident occurred during a joint 10-hour operation with the Road Transport Department (RTD), from 10pm on Thursday to 8am on Friday.
"Throughout the operation, 46 motorcycles were stopped for inspection and 228 summonses were issued for various offences.
"During the operation, several accidents involving six motorcycles occurred, believed to be due to riders attempting to evade inspection," he said.
He said one Yamaha Y15ZR rider was believed to have tried to ram officers before losing control and crashing.
"At the same time, a Honda Wave 125 motorcycle involved in the crash caught fire shortly after the incident," he said.
Azli said the 6 injured riders included school students, higher education students, labourers and food stall workers.
"Four of them were sent to Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital for further treatment," he said.
He said offences detected included failure to stop for inspection, structural and exhaust modifications, missing side mirrors, improper number plates and not wearing helmets.
All motorcycles involved were taken to the Traffic Enforcement Division of the Kuala Terengganu district police headquarters for further action under Section 64 of the Road Transport Act 1987.
"In the same operation, RTD also issued 280 notices of offences and seized 102 motorcycles," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia envoy rejects claims Sabah land on Sebatik now under Jakarta
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Indonesia's ambassador to Malaysia has dismissed reports that a portion of Pulau Sebatik in Sabah has been placed under Indonesian control, describing the claim as inaccurate.
Datuk Raden Mohammad Iman Hascarya Kusumo said the 127.3-hectare area in question remains a "grey area", with discussions over its status still ongoing at a high level.
"I want to emphasise that it is not under Indonesia's authority. It is still a grey area, and discussions are continuing," he said when contacted in Jakarta.
He added that reports from Indonesia claiming otherwise were incorrect and urged clarification to avoid confusion.
The ambassador also said further verification was needed to determine whether the reports stemmed from misinformation or a misunderstanding of facts.
Earlier, Indonesian media quoted presidential chief of staff M Qodari as saying the land had officially become part of Indonesia following diplomatic efforts to resolve land border issues with Malaysia.
However, Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor has also rejected the claim, saying such reports do not reflect the actual outcome of ongoing boundary negotiations between the two countries.
When asked whether further discussions would take place soon, Iman said both sides are still awaiting developments and would provide updates in due course.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian vessel in Strait of Hormuz damaged, unable to depart
Article:
CYBERJAYA: One of seven Malaysian fuel tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz has suffered damage and is unable to depart from a port there, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Of the vessels involved, one has already arrived in Malaysia while another is approaching national waters.
"One has arrived, and another is close, entering Malaysian waters.
"As for the others, although they have been cleared, we are continuing to monitor the situation," he told reporters after Friday prayers at Surau Bomba Cyberjaya.
Anwar said Iran has granted clearance to all seven vessels.
"However, one has encountered technical problems and is unable to leave the port. Given the latest developments, we are facing some challenges," he said.
On Wednesday, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said
two Malaysian vess
els were en route out of the Strait of Hormuz, with one expected to arrive today and the other next week.
Earlier today, it was reported that Malaysian vessel Ocean Thunder would arrive in Pengerang today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MPSEA calls for faster renewable energy implementation in C&I solar segment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Photovoltaic and Sustainable Energy Industry Association (MPSEA) has called on the government to urgently improve the speed and efficiency of renewable energy (RE) implementation, particularly for the commercial and industrial (C&I) solar segment.
In a statement today, the non-profit organisation representing the solar and RE industry in Malaysia said Malaysia already has the policy ambition, market interest, and supporting frameworks to advance the energy transition.
"The challenge today is no longer policy direction, but execution speed. Without immediate improvements in implementation efficiency, Malaysia risks missing the critical window to translate strong policy into real economic, industrial, and energy security gains.
"Recent geopolitical tensions and global supply uncertainties have reinforced a clear reality: energy security can no longer be assumed," it added.
It said for Malaysia, accelerating RE deployment is not only a sustainability priority, but a strategic necessity to reduce exposure and strengthen long-term resilience.
MPSEA noted that its members continue to see strong interest from businesses across manufacturing, plantation agriculture, logistics, retail and other sectors that are ready to adopt solar.
"However, many still face overlapping approvals, uneven timelines, fragmented processes, and insufficient clarity on programme requirements.
It reckoned that the country already has important mechanisms in place; thus, stronger implementation support is needed, such as clearer guidance, smoother coordination, and faster approvals, which are essential to ensure these programmes deliver their full impact on the ground.
The association also urged the government to improve clarity and consistency across RE programmes and approval pathways, particularly for C&I adopters, as one of the ways to strengthen resilience, attract investment and capture the full benefits of the energy transition.
It also proposes to strengthen facilitation support for applicants navigating programme requirements, grid-related processes, and implementation approvals, and build on existing initiatives such as Green Investment Tax Allowance (GITA) and Solar for Rakyat Incentive Scheme (SolaRIS) by ensuring that policy support is matched by practical implementation efficiency.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ocean Thunder now in Malaysian waters, heading towards Pengerang
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: The Ocean Thunder, a Petronas-chartered crude tanker carrying Iraqi Basrah heavy oil, has entered Malaysian waters and is now making its final approach to Pengerang, with its arrival time pushed to 7pm tonight.
Vessel-tracking data from
maritime monitoring systems
showed the very large crude carrier (VLCC) had earlier been expected to arrive at 1.30pm after entering the Singapore Strait, but subsequent movement and operational coordination requirements have shifted its estimated berthing window.
The 274-metre tanker was recorded steaming steadily at 7.4 knots on a 251-degree course, with a 17-metre draught, according to AIS data received at 4.10pm via a roaming signal.
The 251-degree course indicates the Ocean Thunder is on a west-southwest inbound track through the Singapore Strait, aligning itself with Malaysian waters for the final pilotage and berthing approach into Pengerang.
The delay is understood to be linked to a mandatory pilot exchange and port traffic coordination before it can proceed to Jetty 2 at the high-security Petronas facility.
Despite the revised timing, the vessel's progression signals that discharge operations remain on track pending final clearance.
The Ocean Thunder is carrying approximately one million barrels of Iraqi Basrah heavy crude, a dense, high-sulphur grade typically processed at complex refineries such as those in Pengerang to produce higher-value petroleum products.
Its voyage began in early March from Al Basrah, Iraq, before the tanker transited the Strait of Hormuz on April 5.
The strait is facing a critical global chokepoint currently under geopolitical strain amid tensions involving the United States, Israel and Iran.
Only a limited number of Malaysia-linked tankers have successfully navigated the route in recent weeks, reflecting heightened sensitivity in global energy corridors.
The vessel was among those granted passage following diplomatic arrangements, with Iraqi crude reportedly exempted from certain restrictions allowing transit through contested waters.
Another Malaysia-bound tanker, the Serifos, is expected to arrive in Melaka on April 21, according to shipping intelligence databases.
Earlier in Cyberjaya today, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said one Malaysian-linked vessel has already arrived, while another is approaching Malaysian waters after Iran granted passage for seven ships through the Strait of Hormuz, although one remains unable to depart due to damage.
He said the government is closely monitoring the situation amid disruptions at the strategic shipping lane following the Feb 28 attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran.
For now, the Ocean Thunder is no longer just "approaching", it is inside Malaysian waters, crawling steadily toward its final berth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MICCI flags rising freight, insurance costs as Strait of Hormuz tensions strain supply chains
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian International Chamber of Commerce and Industry (MICCI) has warned of rising freight rates and insurance premiums following disruptions linked to a US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, with cost pressures already filtering through global supply chains, though impacts vary across industries.
MICC President Christina Tee said the higher logistics costs are squeezing business margins and cash flow, noting that while some firms may absorb part of the increase in the short term, sustained pressure will eventually require partial cost pass-through to consumers.
She stressed the need for clear, consistent and timely communication from the government to help businesses plan and coordinate amid evolving geopolitical conditions.
"Amid evolving conditions, we underscore the importance of clear, consistent and timely communication from the government to industry stakeholders to enable better planning and coordination," she said.
MICC I am closely monitoring risks around the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a key maritime chokepoint linking Asia and Europe via the Suez Canal, amid broader regional uncertainty.
It noted that while Malaysia's trade with the European Union accounts for about 7.5 per cent of total trade, the key concern lies in the strategic importance of the shipping corridor rather than trade share alone.
Any disruption could impact global shipping flows, including vessel availability, freight rates and overall supply chain reliability. Export-heavy sectors such as electrical and electronics (E&E), manufacturing, commodities and petroleum-related industries are expected to feel more immediate pressure.
MICC I said longer rerouting via the Cape of Good Hope could add 10 to 14 days to transit times, forcing companies to revise production schedules, inventory management and delivery timelines.
Operationally, Malaysian businesses are responding by reviewing inventory levels, building buffer stocks and working with logistics partners to explore alternative shipping routes to minimise delays.
However, MICCI cautioned that while short-term disruptions may be manageable, prolonged issues could place additional strain on supply chains, particularly for time-sensitive shipments.
Companies are also increasingly exploring alternative sourcing channels and supplier diversification, although such adjustments often come with higher costs and operational trade-offs.
Meanwhile, MICCI also called for closer coordination between government and industry to ensure the uninterrupted flow of goods through critical maritime routes.
It has submitted recommendations to the National Economic Action Council, urging a coordinated, data-driven assessment of the crisis, including mapping vulnerable commodities such as energy inputs, food materials, packaging and industrial components.
The chamber also recommended potential support measures such as temporary loan repayment flexibility, insurance relief, tax incentives and improved access to financing if disruptions persist, particularly for vulnerable sectors like manufacturing and food and beverage, which are already facing rising costs and operational challenges.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Factory supervisor charged with murder of nine-month-old boy
Article:
KLANG: A factory supervisor was charged at the magistrate's court here with the murder of a nine-month-old boy earlier this month.
Mohd Syahril Nazrul Ruslim, 37, is accused of causing the infant's death at an apartment unit in Taman Bayu Perdana around 3.30pm on April 6.
The charge was framed under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of up to 40 years and not less than 12 strokes of the cane if the death penalty is not imposed, upon conviction.
No plea was recorded after the charge was read before magistrate A. Khartiyayini, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
The court fixed July 6 for mention of the case and submission of the forensic report.
Deputy public prosecutor Nadia Syuhada Rosli appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Shared responsibility: Anwar says students not exempt from cleaning toilets
Article:
CYBERJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today defended the practice of students cleaning school toilets, describing it as a necessary lesson in responsibility and discipline.
He said the move should not be disputed as it applies to all students, regardless of their family background or social standing.
"I understand that some parents have objected to this on social media. But I would like to advise that all our children, without exception, are not from aristocratic backgrounds.
"We all share responsibility. We use the toilets, so we must clean them," he said after Friday prayers at Surau Bomba Cyberjaya.
Anwar said the task was not difficult and urged parents to cooperate in instilling a sense of accountability in their children.
He said cleaning toilets should not be viewed as the sole responsibility of cleaners.
"Do not regard it as the job of cleaners alone, as though it reinforces class divisions in society," he said.
Anwar had previously directed all schools to introduce toilet-cleaning schedules involving students to ensure better hygiene standards nationwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCBA to be equipped with firearms by year-end, says Home Minister
Article:
PORT KLANG: The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) is set to be equipped with firearms by the end of the year.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said several options are currently being studied, including the use of taser guns and light firearms such as pistols.
"Preliminary groundwork on the procurement had already been initiated, and an official announcement on the matter will be made in the near future.
"The bottom line is that we agree MCBA must be equipped with the necessary weapons," he said during a press conference at the Al-Quran Veneration programme aboard Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency's KM Tun Fatimah, here.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China expected to keep benchmark lending rates steady after strong GDP data
Article:
SHANGHAI: China is expected to leave benchmark lending rates unchanged for an 11th consecutive month in April, a Reuters survey showed, as robust first-quarter growth and a pick-up in inflation have weakened the case for additional monetary stimulus.
This week, China's economy logged 5.0 per cent growth, picking up from 4.5 per cent in the previous quarter, and at the top of its full-year target range.
Even before the gross domestic product (GDP) figures were released, it was evident that the world's No. 2 economy was weathering the Iran war better than others, prompting major investment banks to walk back calls for rate cuts. They now expect China to keep official interest rates steady this year.
The loan prime rate (LPR), normally charged to banks' best clients, is calculated each month after 20 designated commercial banks submit proposed rates to the People's Bank of China (PBOC).
In a Reuters survey of 20 market participants this week, all respondents predicted that at the next review on Monday, the one-year and five-year LPRs would remain steady at 3.00 per cent and 3.50 per cent respectively.
In addition to the upbeat GDP figures, China's factory-gate prices in March turned positive for the first time in more than three years, pointing to rising import cost pressures linked to the Middle East crisis.
"Stronger-than-expected first-quarter GDP data, combined with the recent reflationary trends, may keep the PBOC on hold until conditions warrant monetary policy support," Lynn Song, ING's chief economist for Greater China, said in a note.
Raymond Yeung, chief economist for Greater China at ANZ, noted that keeping rates steady would be "consistent with the PBOC's preference to manage conditions via structural tools rather than rate cuts while growth remains near target."
China's central bank has said it will maintain an "appropriately loose" monetary stance this year, deploying tools including cuts to reserve requirements ‌and interest ⁠rates to keep liquidity ample.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Rafizi's ex-aide James Chai to return on April 28 to assist MACC probe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Political analyst James Chai is expected to return to Malaysia on April 28 to assist the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in its investigation into a RM1.11 billion government-backed semiconductor project.
The investigation involves UK-based Arm Holdings.
MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki confirmed to New Straits Times that Chai's return had been communicated to the commission, adding that investigators had briefed him on the matter.
"I was told that he even purchased his own flight ticket, which he can claim reimbursement with us once he returns.
"We just need him to come back and have his statement recorded to facilitate further investigations. We look forward to his return," he said.
In a statement, law firm Kesavan Advocates and Solicitors, which represents Chai, said arrangements had been made following approval from his employer in London.
The firm said Chai would be available to assist investigators on April 28 at 10am, and that the MACC had confirmed the timing.
It added that Chai has been cooperative throughout and had made himself available to assist investigations so his role could be clarified.
"We have full confidence in the Malaysian criminal justice system… our client was contactable, not evading any investigations, and has made himself available," the firm said.
The MACC had previously sought Chai, who served as an aide to former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, in connection with the probe.
The commission had also
offered to cover his travel costs
to return to Malaysia.
So far, statements have been recorded from 17 witnesses, including officials from the Economy Ministry and agencies such as the Malaysian Investment Development Authority.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ford recalls about 1.4 million pickup trucks over gearshift issue
Article:
NEW YORK: Ford is recalling about 1.4 million F-150 pickup trucks in the US following a US National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) investigation into reports of unexpected downshifts, the regulator said on Friday.
The NHTSA said Ford was aware of two "potentially" related injuries and one accident, and that dealers would update the trucks' powertrain control module software as a remedy.
Earlier this year, the regulator had expanded a safety-related investigation into the issue. A preliminary evaluation was first opened in March last year, after receiving complaints related to 2015–2017 F-150 pickups equipped with the '6R80' transmission.
Ford had earlier said that the issue may have been caused by electrical connections wearing down over time due to heat and vibration, leading to signal loss from the transmission range sensor.
The regulator said that incorrect signals likely lead to an unintended downshift.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK's Workspace warns of 'substantial' profit drop for fiscal 2027, shares fall
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Flexible office-space provider Workspace on Friday warned of a "substantial step down" in fiscal 2027 profits from 2026 amid falling rents and higher costs, sending its shares down nearly 15 per cent.
Total rent roll fell 1.4 per cent to 127.3 million pounds (US$171.93 million) in the three months to March 31, while stabilised portfolio rent per square foot dipped 0.9 per cent to 46.31 pounds.
London-focused Workspace also expects a negative impact on the valuation of its property portfolio in the second half of fiscal 2027.
The company's shares were down 14.1 per cent at 320.6 pence in early trading.
For the fiscal year ended March 31, Workspace said trading profit after interest was seen in line with market expectations, though it did not provide details.
The company has been under pressure in recent years as businesses delayed leasing decisions or curtailed office-space budgets amid rising hybrid working, prompting Workspace to expand its core business and focus on customer retention.
However, with companies now increasingly asking employees to return to offices, demand for flexible and permanent spaces is likely to pick up. A Savills survey showed 85 per cent of global flex operators expect to expand in 2026.
"As we deliberately reposition the business, there will be a step down in profitability," CEO Charlie Green said. Green took the helm in February amid pressure to improve performance and calls from activist investor Saba Capital to wind down the business.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khaled: Lebanon-Israel ceasefire a step forward, but risks remain
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel marks a positive step towards easing tensions, but the situation on the ground remains uncertain, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said.
He noted reports of several ceasefire violations in southern Lebanon, underscoring the fluid security environment.
"The Defence Ministry's priority remains focused on the safety of Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 personnel, who are carrying out their responsibilities under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) mission," he said in a statement.
A 10-day ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel took effect yesterday, as US President Donald Trump said efforts were underway to arrange a first-ever meeting between the leaders of the two countries.
The truce, which began at midnight local time, comes as Washington intensifies efforts to secure a broader agreement to end the conflict involving Iran, with Iran insisting that a Lebanon truce be part of any deal.
Khaled said all Malaysian personnel have been reported safe and continue to carry out their duties in line with their mandate.
He added that the ministry, through the Joint Forces Headquarters, would continue to monitor developments closely in coordination with Unifil and relevant parties.
"Any operational adjustments will be made based on Unifil directives and assessments on the ground," he said.
Malbatt remains on high alert to respond to any developments and ensure the safety of its personnel, he added.
Khaled also urged all parties to respect the ceasefire to prevent renewed escalation, expressing hope it could pave the way for a lasting resolution through diplomatic channels.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: French train maker Alstom hit by delivery delays
Article:
PARIS: Alstom shares plummeted Friday after the French train maker warned that "execution headwinds" had delayed deliveries to clients, forcing the group to abandon its profit and cash flow targets.
The company unveiled the setbacks late Thursday while reporting a four percent rise in sales for its financial year to end-March, but a dip in operating margins to around six percent.
"In a business where rigorous planning and disciplined execution are essential, some large rolling-stock projects have progressed more slowly than anticipated, weighing on near-term margins and cash," Alstom's chief executive officer Martin Sion, who took up his new post this month, said in a statement.
"We are therefore launching immediate actions to stabilise performance, while preparing deeper operational changes to restore sustainable execution, cash generation and profitable growth."
Free cash flow dropped to around 330 million euros for its full year, compared with 502 million the year before, and Alstom said it would not meet its goal of a total cash flow of 1.5 billion euros in the three years to March 2027.
It also said profit margins would not improve to 8-10 per cent next year as promised.
The financial warnings rattled investors, with Alstom shares dropping nearly 30 per cent to 16.13 euros on the Paris stock exchange.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nestlé Malaysia hits 100pct local chilli sourcing milestone for Maggi sauce
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Nestlé Malaysia has achieved a major milestone in its local sourcing efforts, with 100 per cent of the fresh chillies used in its Maggi Chilli Sauce now sourced from within the country.
The achievement underscores the company's long-term commitment to strengthening national food security while uplifting local farming communities.
The milestone was highlighted during an official visit by Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir to the People's Income Initiative-Agropreneur (IPR-INTAN) Putrajaya chilli farming project.
The visit showcased how structured partnerships between the private sector, government agencies and smallholder farmers can drive sustainable agriculture while creating stable livelihoods for B40 communities.
During the visit, the minister was briefed on the success of the high-yield chilli farming initiative, which has significantly improved farmer incomes, productivity and integration into Nestlé Malaysia's supply chain.
The project, which operates under the Nestlé Chilli Club since 1995, supports farmers across 12 sites in Kelantan, Selangor, Putrajaya and Johor, spanning across 146 acres.
By providing structured training, guaranteed demand and fully traceable supply chains, the programme ensures income stability and long-term growth for participating farmers, and after years of expansion, it now fully meets the chilli requirements for Maggi Chilli Sauce.
Nestlé Malaysia chief executive officer Juan Aranols emphasised the broader significance of the initiative, noting that supporting local farmers is essential not only to the company's operations but also to the country's food security agenda.
"Long-term partnerships with farming communities and government stakeholders, including our collaboration with IPR INTAN under the ministry, reflect our shared responsibility to build a more resilient and self-sufficient food system while empowering B40 households," he added.
Farmers involved in the IPR-INTAN Putrajaya project currently earn an average monthly income of about RM3,000.
Now in its fifth cycle in 2026, the initiative has produced over 200 tonnes of chillies valued at about RM1.5 million, demonstrating the economic potential of structured smallholder farming.
Nestlé Malaysia further supports farmers through training in modern, eco-friendly regenerative agricultural practices aimed at improving yields and productivity.
The chilli initiative forms part of Nestlé Malaysia's broader Farmer Connect programmes, which also support rice, cocoa and coffee farmers nationwide.
These programmes focus on strengthening farm productivity and resilience through regenerative agriculture, technical training and long-term capacity building, including collaborations with the Malaysian Cocoa Board in Sabah and Sarawak and coffee farming in Kedah as well as Gua Musa, Kelantan.
Nestlé Malaysia aims to continue expanding local sourcing while building a more sustainable and resilient food ecosystem for the future.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Real estate transaction scams: What buyers need to know
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Buying or selling property is one of the largest financial transactions in a person's life. Whether buying a first home, investing in property, or selling an existing asset, the process involves large sums of money and complex legal documents.
However, this also opens the door to various forms of fraud that can cause significant losses to victims.
Impersonating real estate agents
Fraud by individuals posing as real estate agents is becoming more common and often targets victims who are looking for affordable housing.
In most cases, scammers will offer properties at prices far below market prices to attract attention, then urge victims to make a deposit payment immediately on the grounds of high demand.
To avoid becoming a victim, the public should always be alert to suspicious signs such as agents refusing to meet, failure to show valid documents, or requests for payment to a personal account.
As a preventive measure, consumers are advised to check the status of agents through the valuation board or real estate agents. In addition, avoid making any payments before physically viewing the property and signing a valid written agreement.
If fraud has occurred, victims should immediately contact their bank and file a report with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) or the National Fraud Response Centre (NSRC) to increase the chances of further action being taken.
Being cautious and not rushing is the key to protecting yourself from becoming a victim of real estate fraud.
There are also cases of forgery of real estate documents. Fraudsters can forge land grants, sale and purchase agreements or owner identity documents.
With these fake documents, they try to convince buyers that the transaction is legitimate.
Without careful checking, buyers may be fooled and make payments before realising the fraud.
To overcome cases of forgery of real estate documents, buyers need to take precautions by conducting thorough checks before proceeding with any transaction.
Among them, ensure that the validity of the land grant and ownership status is checked through authorities such as the Land and Mines Office. In addition, use the services of a certified real estate lawyer to examine each document, including the sale and purchase agreement, so that any invalidity can be detected early.
Buyers are also advised to verify the identity of the seller and ensure that the transaction is carried out face-to-face, not just through online communication.
In addition, avoid making payments directly to individuals without going through official channels such as a law firm account.
Fake property ads
Scams can also occur through fake property ads on the internet. Scammers will use pictures of real homes taken from other websites and promote them as properties for sale or rent.
They will then ask for a reservation fee or deposit supposedly to 'lock' the deal.
Before making any payments, make sure to physically visit the property and avoid paying a deposit just to 'lock' the deal. If an offer seems too good to be true, it is most likely a scam.
Double sale scams
Apart from fake agent scams, forged documents and fake property advertisements, there are several other types of scams in the property sector that you should be aware of.
One of these is the double sale scam, where the seller sells the same property to more than one buyer at the same time.
Victims usually only become aware of this when the transfer of ownership process cannot be completed. There are also cases of non-existent rental scams, where the fraudster poses as a landlord and rents out a property that they do not actually own, then disappears after receiving the deposit.
Non-existent or abandoned housing projects
In addition, there are also non-existent or abandoned housing project scams, where irresponsible developers offer projects that look convincing but in fact have not received approval from the authorities or have never been started.
In this case, buyers are encouraged to check the status of the project with the Ministry of Housing and Local Government before making any payment.
Buyers should be careful in payment transactions, as most frauds occur when money is transferred without proper verification.
Any deposit, booking or initial instalment payments should be made only after documents are verified and the transaction is handled through legal channels.
Buyers are encouraged to use a law firm account or official payment method that can be recorded, rather than transferring money to an individual's personal account whose status is unclear.
Keep all receipts, proof of transactions, chat messages and copies of documents, as all of these are very important in the event of a dispute or fraud report in the future.
Buyers should also not make hasty decisions just for fear of missing out on the opportunity to own a property that is said to be 'cheap' or 'limited'.
Fraudsters often use pressure tactics such as demanding immediate payment or giving a short period of time to influence the victim.
In such situations, buyers need to stay calm and seek the opinion of family members, lawyers or real estate experts before making a large financial commitment.
Patience, thoroughness and rationality are essential so that every decision is made based on facts and not just emotions.
In conclusion, awareness and knowledge are the main keys to avoiding fraud in real estate transactions.
By conducting thorough checks and seeking advice from certified professionals, the risk of becoming a victim of fraud can be significantly reduced. - The author is a lawyer at J A Nabila & Co.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: British PM under renewed fire over ex-envoy to US appointment
Article:
LONDON: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer faced mounting pressure on Friday over his appointment of Peter Mandelson as envoy to Washington, after it emerged the veteran politician had failed security vetting.
The affair has dogged Starmer for months amid anger over Mandelson's 2024 nomination to the diplomatic post despite his reported ties to late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
Mandelson, a long-time figure in the Labour Party, has held multiple ministerial roles since the late 1990s but has been repeatedly linked to scandals throughout his career.
The government said foreign ministry officials were responsible for the latest failure, adding that neither Starmer nor his foreign minister were informed of the failed security check until this week.
The top civil servant at the foreign ministry, Olly Robbins, has since resigned after Starmer "lost confidence in him" for failing to disclose the matter, senior minister Darren Jones said on Friday.
Foreign ministry officials had allowed Mandelson's appointment to proceed "against the recommendation of UK Security Vetting", the government said.
Opposition calls for Starmer's resignation intensified after reports that Mandelson was appointed despite failing background security checks.
The ambassador to Washington is regarded as Britain's most important diplomatic post, responsible for managing the so-called "special relationship" with US President Donald Trump.
Jones said it was "unacceptable" that the vetting advice had been overridden and described the situation as "a failure of the state", while denying Starmer had misled parliament.
He said Starmer would address parliament on Monday and insisted his position was not at risk.
Opposition Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said on X that "Starmer has betrayed our national security. He should go."
Police have also opened an investigation into allegations involving Mandelson, who was arrested and released on bail in February. He has not been charged and denies wrongdoing.
The investigation reportedly concerns claims he leaked sensitive documents to Epstein while serving as a government minister, including during the 2008 financial crisis.
Lawmakers have ordered the release of thousands of documents relating to Mandelson's vetting, with a further batch expected in the coming weeks.
—AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lebanese trickle home as ceasefire with Israel mostly holds
Article:
BEIRUT: People displaced by the war in Lebanon began returning home on Friday to check on their properties, though some remained cautious, saying a fragile ceasefire between Hezbollah and Israel may not hold.
Hills of rubble marked the site of destroyed buildings in Beirut's southern suburbs, a Hezbollah-controlled area heavily bombarded by Israel during more than six weeks of conflict linked to the wider regional war involving the United States and Iran.
In Qasmiyeh in southern Lebanon, cars crossed a makeshift bridge over the Litani River, hastily set up after the ceasefire came into effect at midnight local time (2100 GMT). Israel had destroyed all bridges over the river during the war, including one at Qasmiyeh on Thursday.
"I inspected my home and, praise God, the building is still standing," said Ali Hamza, who had visited his house in the southern suburbs known as Dahiyeh.
But he said conditions remained difficult.
"People are scared to come and live, and it is impossible to live in these circumstances and with these smells. A full return is difficult now despite the hardship of displacement," he said.
The ceasefire was announced by US President Donald Trump on Thursday between the governments of Lebanon and Israel. However, the Lebanese army reported alleged violations, including intermittent shelling in southern villages, and urged residents to delay returning.
There was no immediate comment from the Israeli military.
In the largely destroyed southern city of Nabatieh, some residents said they would stay despite the devastation, while others said there was nothing left to return to.
"There's destruction and it's unliveable. We're taking our things and leaving again," said Fadel Badreddine, who had returned with his wife and young son.
The war has killed more than 2,100 people in Lebanon and displaced around 1.2 million, according to Lebanese authorities. Hezbollah attacks killed two Israeli civilians and 13 Israeli soldiers since March 2, Israel said.
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he had not agreed to withdraw forces from southern Lebanon and would maintain a "security zone" near the border with Syria. Hezbollah, meanwhile, said any ceasefire must not allow Israel freedom of movement inside Lebanon.
Israeli forces have previously pushed into parts of southern Lebanon and ordered residents to evacuate areas south of the Litani River during the conflict, saying the aim was to create a buffer zone to protect northern Israel.
The 1948 conflict between Israel and Lebanon remains formally unresolved.
—Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heartbreak to handbreak: love spat turns violent
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A dispute believed to have stemmed from a love triangle turned violent at Karpal Singh Drive, police said.
Northeast district deputy police chief Lee Swee Sake said a report was lodged on April 10, with seven individuals arrested to assist in investigations.
"We have received the report, and as of now, seven arrests have been made," he said.
Video footage of the incident has since gone viral.
Lee added that investigation papers have been referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor, and police are awaiting further instructions.
"Preliminary investigations indicate that the fight was triggered by a long-standing grudge involving a dispute over a girlfriend," he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 147 of the Penal Code for rioting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Apple's iPhone shipments in China surge 20pct in Q1, data shows
Article:
BEIJING: Apple's iPhone shipments surged 20 per cent in China in the first quarter, for the strongest growth among major vendors, despite an overall decline as rising prices of memory chips boosted costs, data from Counterpoint Research showed.
Overall smartphone shipments dropped 4.0 per cent in the world's largest smartphone market in the period from January to March, hit by supply chain disruptions and the soaring chip prices.
But China's two largest smartphone vendors, telecoms giant Huawei and Apple, bucked the trend, reporting growth of 2.0 per cent and 20 per cent respectively.
"As most rivals raise prices, Apple stands out for value, with Chinese consumers knowing its products last at least three years," Ivan Lam, senior analyst at Counterpoint Research.
Huawei's shipments were lifted by strong demand across both its high-end and budget ranges, including the Enjoy 90 series, giving it a 20 per cent market share in the quarter, Lam said.
Huawei retained the top spot, followed by Apple with a share of 19 per cent.
Smartphone vendors in China have raised prices for budget handsets to protect margins in the battle with elevated memory chip costs.
Xiaomi slipped to sixth place with a plunge of 35 per cent in shipments. Lam attributed the sharp decline to a high base effect, after Xiaomi benefited from aggressive price cuts and government subsidies in the corresponding period last year.
Shipments by Oppo and Honor also fell 5.0 per cent and 3.0 per cent respectively, though Vivo saw a rise of 2.0 per cent, buoyed by strong sales during the Lunar New Year holiday.
Lam expected more headwinds for the market in the second quarter, particularly as Chinese brands look to raise prices further.
"However, we expect Apple and Huawei to fare relatively better, with Huawei potentially seeing further shipment growth driven by solid demand for its lower-end devices," Lam said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman convicted of murdering brother with boyfriend over phone dispute
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A woman has been convicted by the High Court of murdering her elder brother with her boyfriend in Taiping over a mobile phone dispute seven years ago.
Both accused cannot be named as they were minors at the time of the offence.
They were ordered to be detained at the pleasure of the Ruler of Perak under Section 97(2) of the Child Act.
Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin found the woman and her boyfriend, who were aged 14 and 15 at the time of the incident, guilty of murder with common intention under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which carries the death penalty.
According to the facts of the case, the victim was killed after he got into a fight with his sister's boyfriend, who had wanted to retrieve his handphone left at the girl's house.
The victim, who had answered a call on the phone, asked his sister's boyfriend to come to the house to collect the phone before a fight ensued that ended in murder.
Evidence indicated that the victim had been incapacitated before being restrained with his hands tied behind his back during the attack on July 5, 2019.
The victim's decomposing body was discovered two days later behind a neighbouring unoccupied house, wrapped in a grey comforter and a plastic bag, with his hands still tied behind his back.
A post-mortem examination revealed that the cause of death was excessive bleeding from a stab wound to the neck that penetrated the trachea.
Defensive injuries on the victim's hands indicated that he struggled during the attack.
Ruwena, in her grounds of judgment dated April 16, said although no one witnessed the killing, the circumstantial evidence formed a complete chain pointing to the guilt of both accused.
The court further found that the victim had likely been incapacitated before being tied up, suggesting premeditation.
"The victim bled to a slow death because the fatal injuries did not cut the main artery.
"I cannot imagine the pain, despair and helplessness that the deceased must have felt as he slowly bled to death.
"The tragedy of it all was that the accused and the deceased were siblings who grew up together and were close, according to the eldest sibling.
"Their murderous acts were very serious and I would not have hesitated to impose the death sentence upon them if they had been adults when the crime was committed," she said.
The court also dismissed both accused's application to be released on a bond of good behaviour as there were no special circumstances to justify the order.
"The first accused (man) pleaded that he was remorseful and hoped to continue his studies.
"The second accused (woman) also sought leniency, saying she is now married and has a young child.
"However, the court noted that she appeared to show little remorse for her role in the killing of her brother, despite previously describing him as someone close.
"The court must show its abhorrence at such a grave and vicious crime committed against her brother, in which a life was lost tragically and unnecessarily," she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MOHE ready to place students affected by Middle East crisis in local universities
Article:
JASIN: The government is prepared to place Malaysian students affected by the ongoing crisis in the Middle East into local higher education institutions if the need arises.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said the move is part of the ministry's contingency plan to ensure students can continue their studies without disruption, including those who have recently received offers to study in affected countries.
"If it happens, space will be provided for them to continue their studies here, in accordance with their results, courses, and educational background, as there is understanding at the university level," he told reporters after attending a town hall programme at Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) Jasin.
He said discussions would typically focus on credit transfers based on guidelines set by the Malaysian Qualifications Agency to avoid students having to repeat their studies.
Zambry added that the approach is not new and has been implemented in past crises, including during the Iran–Iraq conflict and involving Malaysian students in Kuwait.
He said there are no official figures on affected students, as universities in the region continue to operate, with numbers generally low except in countries such as Jordan and Egypt.
"However, the ministry will remain prepared and await feedback from students and educational institutions before taking further action," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] UM refutes claims of 'cancer cluster' caused by hospital SOP breaches
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Malaya (UM) has refuted claims linking radiation from a PET-CT machine at the UM Medical Centre to cancer cases among several staff members.
"The safety, health and well-being of all staff and the public remain a priority at UMMC.
"In response to allegations linking the PET-CT scanning facility to harmful radiation risks, the management wishes to stress that the claims are untrue," it said in a statement.
UM said PET-CT scan operations are conducted according to strict safety standards, and radiation exposure levels are controlled within established limits.
It added that all radiation workers are required to wear personal dosimeters.
"Official records show that worker radiation exposure is significantly lower than the gazetted dose limits."
It added that periodic medical check-ups are also conducted according to a fixed schedule.
UM said it complies with the Radiation Protection Program Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), the Atomic Energy Licensing Act, and relevant 2010 and 2011 radiation protection and waste management regulations.
"Radioactive waste management is closely monitored and implemented with the approval of relevant authorities.
"An inspection by the Health Ministrys Medical Radiation Regulatory Division in Oct 2025 found no procedural violations, confirming that UMMC complied with all set regulations.".
Annual license inspection visits by the dvisions enforcement officers are also carried out to ensure ongoing compliance, it said.
UM said it remains ready for further inspections by authorities to ensure continued adherence to safety standards.
Earlier today, activist group #ReformIPT called for a probe into alleged radiation safety breaches at UMMC, claiming negligence led to staff developing chronic illnesses.
The group alleged that improper installation of PET-CT scanners at the hospital's Nuclear Medicine Unit resulted in personnel contracting lung, blood, and breast cancers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-prisoner jailed after exposing himself to woman in Klang
Article:
KLANG: An unemployed man has been jailed for two months after pleading guilty to exposing himself to a woman.
Magistrate A. Karthiyayini sentenced Muhammad Rakis Abdul Lama, 38, after he admitted the charge on Friday.
He was charged under Section 509 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum jail term of five years, a fine, or both.
The court ordered the sentence to run from his arrest on April 14.
According to the charge, the man, who had recently been released from prison, insulted the modesty of a 21-year-old woman by exposing himself at a house in Teluk Pulai, Klang, on April 12.
He watched the victim in the living room before lying on his side, facing her and exposing himself.
The woman screamed and called her brother-in-law, who was upstairs, for help.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid Hamidi: Election readiness primary goal for Melaka Umno meet tonight
Article:
MELAKA, April 17 — Planning and preparations for the upcoming General Election (GE) and State Elections will be the primary focus of the Umno Melaka meeting scheduled for tonight, said party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
He said the meeting, which will bring together Umno and Barisan Nasional leadership, will serve as an initial platform to coordinate the party machinery and strengthen the party's grassroots organisation ahead of the polls.
"This is the first of the party's meetings in the state as part of our preparations for the upcoming elections," he said during the simultaneous opening ceremony of Umno Branch Meetings for Melaka at the Melaka International Trade Centre (MITC) here today.
Also present were Umno Secretary-General Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, Umno Youth Chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh, and Melaka Umno Liaison Committee Chairman Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh. — Bernama

Title: Anwar orders study on restoring Langkawi’s duty-free status, to review EV policies following BYD plant concerns
Article:
CYBERJAYA, April 17 — The government will study the proposal to restore Langkawi’s duty-free status as a move to revitalise the island’s economy, which is currently facing challenges from rising costs and a decline in domestic tourism appeal.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the proposal deserves attention as Langkawi’s tourism sector has been a bit sluggish lately.
“I have asked the Minister of Finance II (Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan) to take an overall look and see how it can be regenerated because costs in Langkawi are quite high, and that in itself cannot attract tourists, even domestic ones,” he told reporters after performing Friday prayers at the Cyberjaya Fire and Rescue Department Surau here today.
Anwar also expressed his appreciation to the Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, for the proposal.
“This means we are prepared to study the matter and respect the views of Tuanku Sultan of Kedah, and we will examine it,” he said.
On April 13, Sultan Sallehuddin, during the opening ceremony of the Kedah State Legislative Assembly, called on the Federal Government to consider restoring Langkawi’s duty-free privileges to revitalise the island’s tourism sector and local economy.
In another development, Anwar said the issue of allegedly strict conditions imposed on electric vehicle (EV) companies would be brought up for discussion with the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (MITI).
Yesterday, Perak Tourism, Industry, Investment and Corridor Development Committee chairman Loh Sze Yee was reported to have said that the Federal Government needs to reassess the conditions imposed on EV companies planning to set up factories in the country.
Loh said there were concerns about several policies that were too stringent and could undermine foreign investor confidence, thus damaging the country’s investment climate.
He also said that the construction of an EV assembly plant by BYD in Tanjung Malim has been halted, with the existing site currently inactive, believed to be due to the company’s inability to meet new requirements introduced by MITI. — Bernama

Title: No let-up for KLCC ‘photo touts’ as DBKL seizes more cameras in latest ‘Op Lens’ raid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has intensified its crackdown on unauthorised photographers, or ‘photo touts,’ around the city’s top tourist spots, seizing more equipment and issuing fresh compounds in its latest multi-agency raid on Thursday.
The operation, dubbed “Op Lens,” targeted public gathering areas around the Saloma Link and the KLCC esplanade.
It was conducted jointly with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Immigration Department (JIM), and the National Registration Department (NRD).
During the Thursday raid, enforcement officers issued five compound notices to individuals found operating without a license.
They also carried out four seizures of various photography equipment for offences under the Hawker Licensing By-Laws (WPKL) 2016.
Additionally, one photographer who had failed to pay previous fines was served a notice to appear in court.
This latest enforcement action follows a similar raid conducted on Monday, where DBKL issued four compound notices, seized another four sets of photography equipment, and served two court appearance notices for unpaid fines.
In a statement, DBKL reiterated its commitment to clearing the area of illegal photography services, citing reasons such as security risks, public disturbance, and traffic obstruction caused by the touts.
“DBKL will continue to monitor and carry out enforcement actions from time to time to ensure the area remains safe from illegal unauthorised photography service activities,” the statement read.
All seized equipment has been transferred to the DBKL Seizure Store for documentation.
Members of the public are encouraged to channel any complaints or feedback through official DBKL communication channels.

Title: Widespread thunderstorm warning hits KL, Selangor, Penang; Friday commuters brace for heavy rain
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a severe thunderstorm warning for large swathes of Peninsular Malaysia and parts of Sarawak, effective immediately until 7.00pm this evening.
The alert, issued at 4.40pm, warns of thunderstorms, heavy rainfall, and strong winds, impacting the peak Friday evening commute for millions of residents in major urban centres.
Key areas affected include the entirety of Perak and Penang, along with most districts in Selangor and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur.
The warning also extends to Perlis, nearly all of Kedah, parts of Negeri Sembilan, and several districts in Johor, including Johor Bahru and Kota Tinggi.
On the east coast of the peninsula, residents in parts of Kelantan, Terengganu, and most of Pahang are also advised to take precautions.
In East Malaysia, the warning covers several divisions in Sarawak, including Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, and parts of Miri and Limbang.
MetMalaysia issues such a warning when there are signs of thunderstorms with rainfall intensity exceeding 20 mm per hour, which are expected to last for more than an hour.
These conditions can lead to potential hazards such as flash floods in low-lying areas, fallen trees, and reduced visibility on the roads.
Motorists are strongly advised to exercise extreme caution, delay travel if possible, and stay updated through official MetMalaysia channels.

Title: Zara Qairina inquest: Forensic analyst extracts WhatsApp chats, voice notes from seized phone
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, April 17 — The Coroner’s Court here has heard that WhatsApp chats and voice notes were extracted from a mobile phone tagged WF11, one of the exhibits in the case involving Zara Qairina Mahathir’s death.
WF11 was among 15 mobile phones analysed by the Computer Crimes Investigation Unit.
When questioned by conducting officer Sofia S Sawayan, Computer Crimes Investigation Unit analyst ASP Mohd Zaidi Abu Hassan testified that WF11 is an exhibit consisting of a mobile phone and a SIM card.
When the 70th witness was referred to several printouts, he testified that they were from his analysis report of WF11.
“At pages 77, 79, 122, 124-125, 164, and 308 were WhatsApp chats and two voice notes of two teachers.
“At pages 123 was a chat description, while at pages 165 and 942 were text messages sent by the WF11 owner,” explained the witness.
Mohd Zaidi said he identified all the mobile phones including the phones’ SIM cards for the purpose of marking these items as court exhibits.
To a question, the witness explained that the mobile phones were returned by his personnel to the investigating officer of the inquest in the same condition as when he received them.
The inquest resumes on May 18.
Zara, 13, was found under her dormitory building on July 16, 2025 and died the next day in hospital.
Her remains were exhumed on August 9, 2025 and a postmortem was performed on August 10 at the Department of Forensic Medicine, Queen Elizabeth Hospital.
On August 20, 2025, five minors, who were give the pseudonyms Student A, B, C, D, and E, were charged with using insulting words against Zara. — The Borneo Post

Title: Indonesian envoy denies report claiming 127ha of Sebatik shifted to Indonesia, says area still a ‘grey zone’
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Indonesia’s ambassador to Malaysia Datuk Raden Mohammad Iman Hascarya Kusumo has denied reports from Indonesian media alleging that part of Pulau Sebatik in Sabah is now under Indonesia’s jurisdiction.
He told
Berita Harian
that the 127.3‑hectare area mentioned in the reports remains a “grey area” and that discussions to determine its status have not been concluded.
“I want to emphasise that (Pulau Sebatik) is not under Indonesia’s authority, it is still a ‘grey area’,” he said.
“Reports from Indonesia are not correct. Please clarify that the news is not true,” he added when contacted in Jakarta.
He said his office is seeking clearer information to determine whether the reports were false or whether the individual quoted had misstated the facts.
“I need to check the source because many things are unclear, but in any case it is not true,” he said.
Earlier, Indonesian media reported that Presidential Chief of Staff M. Qodari claimed that 127.3 hectares on Pulau Sebatik, previously within Malaysian territory, had officially become part of Indonesia following land border negotiations between the two countries.
Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor has since
rejected the claim
, saying the reports published in Indonesia were inaccurate and did not reflect the actual outcome of Malaysia–Indonesia border discussions.
When asked whether further talks would be held soon to determine the status of the area, Raden Mohammad Iman said they were still awaiting developments.
“We are still waiting on the matter and will provide updates when available,” he said.
Recommended reading:
Sabah CM denies Sebatik land transfer claim amid conflicting reports from Indonesia
Foreign minister says boundary talks progress with neighbours, but Malaysia holds line on China and 1979 Map

Title: Man, 37, faces murder charge in death of nine-month-old baby in Klang
Article:
KLANG, April 17 — A factory supervisor was charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a nine-month-old boy earlier this month.
Mohd Syahril Nazrul Ruslim, 37, is accused of causing the death of the infant at an apartment unit in Taman Bayu Perdana here at about 3.30 pm on April 6.
The charge was framed under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of up to 40 years and not less than 12 strokes of the cane if the death penalty is not imposed, upon conviction.
No plea was recorded from the accused after the charge was read before Magistrate A. Khartiyayini, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
The court then fixed July 6 for mention of the case and submission of the forensic report.
Deputy public prosecutor Nadia Syuhada Rosli appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented. — Bernama

Title: Amazon Web Services to expand presence in Malaysia as Anwar urges more local companies to join in
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Malaysia’s infrastructure strength and supply chain efficiency are among the key factors behind the success of Amazon Web Services’ (AWS) investments in the country, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
In a Facebook post today, he emphasised that AWS should continue expanding opportunities for local companies to participate more meaningfully in its global ecosystem.
“The government will continue strengthening existing policies to ensure long-term benefits from this strategic investment, while also encouraging broader collaboration between local suppliers and their data centre and cloud technology ecosystem,” he said.
Anwar made these remarks after receiving a courtesy visit from AWS Vice President for Public Policy Michael Punke and his delegation, in conjunction with the one-year milestone of the AWS Region launch in Malaysia- a strategic investment that strengthens the country’s position as a regional digital hub.
He added that discussions focused on the progress of AWS investments and their impact on the national economy, particularly in accelerating the digital transformation agenda and cloud adoption under the Madani Government, developing local talent, and commitments to sustainability. — Bernama

Title: Agriculture Ministry welcomes Selangor’s RM131m plan to boost food sector resilience
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 17 — The Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (KPKM) has welcomed the Selangor government’s initiative to introduce the Selangor Resilience Enhancement Package, which involves an allocation of RM131 million, to address current challenges, especially the impact of the global energy crisis on production costs in the agro-food sector.
Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said that the Selangor state government’s commitment reflected the collective approach needed in the current situation.
“A comprehensive approach involving close cooperation between the federal and state governments is essential to ensure that assistance can be delivered more efficiently and comprehensively.
“The ministry appreciates the Selangor government’s commitment to allocate RM25 million for the agricultural sector, which is expected to benefit more than 17,000 padi farmers, fishermen, breeders and aquaculture entrepreneurs in the state,” he said in a statement today.
Mohamad said that the move was in line with and complemented various initiatives by the federal government through KPKM in assisting target groups nationwide.
He said that this included raising the Ploughing Incentive for Padi Farmers (IPKP) to up to RM300 per hectare and BUDI Agri-Commodity cash assistance of up to RM400 per month for farmers and smallholders, as well as the implementation of targeted diesel subsidies for the agro-food sector.
“This synergy will not only strengthen the resilience of the country’s food supply chain, but also ensure the stability of food supply from production to the consumer,” he said.
In this regard, Mohamad said that KPKM welcomed more state governments to come forward and take proactive steps in assisting target groups in the agro-food sector.
This collaborative approach is very important to ensure that industry players remain resilient and to safeguard that the country’s food security, he added.
Mohamad also said he was optimistic that combined efforts between the federal and state governments would have a significant impact in safeguarding the welfare of agro-food industry players and ensuring that the country’s food security would continue to be safeguarded.
The media previously reported that Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said RM130.42 million had been allocated for the first phase of the package, which would commence in June following the state government’s annual budget review and the implementation of several cost-saving measures.
He said the package aimed to strengthen food security, reduce the cost of living, curb excessive inflation, encourage the use of public transport and provide targeted assistance to the most affected groups. — Bernama

Title: Sabah CM denies Sebatik land transfer claim amid conflicting reports from Indonesia
Article:
LAHAD DATU, April 17 — Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor has denied that 127.3 hectares on Pulau Sebatik, previously within Malaysian territory, now belongs to Indonesia.
He said reports of the claim, published by Indonesian media outlets, were inaccurate and did not reflect the actual outcome of border negotiations between Malaysia and Indonesia.
“That is not true. Who said it was handed over?
“Who said it? Indonesian media? That is not true,” he said.
Both Malaysia and Indonesia share Pulau Sebatik, off the coast of Tawau.
Earlier, Warisan President Datuk Seri Panglima Mohd Shafie Apdal said conflicting reports by Indonesia and the Sabah government on the Sebatik border required clarification.
He said increasingly specific statements from Jakarta have not been matched by clear or detailed responses from the Malaysian side.
“Sabahans are entitled to know what discussions have taken place, what has been agreed, and whether any position has been adopted on their behalf,” he said in a statement.
Shafie said he had raised the matter in Parliament as it involves sovereignty, territorial integrity and Sabah’s constitutional rights.
He cited Article 2(b) of the Federal Constitution, stating that any alteration of State boundaries requires the consent of the State Legislative Assembly and the Conference of Rulers.
He said this constitutional requirement must be observed in any matter involving boundary changes.
Shafie said discussions and negotiations between countries do not in themselves constitute an agreement.
He also said any decision affecting Sabah’s territory must be presented to and debated in the Sabah Legislative Assembly.
Earlier, Indonesian media reported that Presidential Chief of Staff M. Qodari claimed that 127.3 hectares on Pulau Sebatik had officially become part of Indonesia following land border diplomacy between the two nations.
Qodari reportedly added that while the settlement resulted in some Indonesian land falling under Malaysian territory, the area involved was only 4.9 hectares — significantly smaller than the land allegedly gained by Indonesia.
Hajiji said Malaysia actually benefited more from the border negotiations involving the two countries.
“We (actually) gained, but I, alongside Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and (Indonesian President) Pak Prabowo Subianto, cannot issue a formal statement yet... that is all I can say,” he added.
International media previously reported that three villages in the Nunukan Regency of North Kalimantan were allegedly included in Malaysian territory following the realignment of the border.
The three villages — Kampung Kabungalor, Kampung Lipaga, and Kampung Tetagas — are located near the Tawau border in Sabah.
The realignment reportedly involves 4.91 hectares of residential land and 127 hectares of oil palm plantations, which are now declared to be within Malaysian territory. — Daily Express

Title: ‘Let’s see’: Anwar doesn’t rule out minister pay cuts if energy crisis escalates
Article:
CYBERJAYA, April 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today he is not discounting suggestions that ministers take a pay cut if the global energy crisis worsens and raises pressure on government spending.
Still, Anwar told reporters here he would prefer the discussion focus on Cabinet members’ performance instead, arguing that their salaries are already comparatively lower.
“Relatively, their salaries are already lower, so the more important thing now is that they perform,” he said in response to a question from the press.
“I don’t take my salary, but their salary is their right, and I think for as long as they do their job well, it’s not unreasonable (they continue to be paid),” he added.
“But let’s see. If the economy does get worse, we can consider it.”
MORE TO COME

Title: Malaysia’s first womb surgery for spine condition sees baby doing well after birth
Article:
KUCHING, April 17 — A baby who underwent in-utero fetal surgery to repair spina bifida at Sarawak General Hospital (SGH) here in September last year is now in good condition.
Health Minister Dzulkefly Ahmad said the four-month-old infant is growing well, with clinical assessments showing the baby is able to breathe, swallow and pass urine and stool normally.
“More remarkably, the baby’s legs are moving actively and strongly, and the infant did not require a ventriculoperitoneal (VP) shunt after birth,” he said in a Facebook post today.
VP shunt is a procedure to drain excess fluid from the brain to the abdomen, helping to relieve pressure and prevent damage caused by fluid build-up.
Dr Dzulkefly said that during his visit to SGH yesterday, he met the team of specialists whom had made national history by performing the surgery, the first of its kind in Malaysia.
